seoTitle: How to Create a Content Calendar in Trello
metaDescription: Launch a content calendar in Trello using the built-in editorial template, then customize labels, dates, and the Calendar view for publishing.
intro: Building a content calendar from scratch takes time you'd rather spend writing. This guide covers using Trello's pre-built editorial template to instantly set up a content pipeline, then customizing labels and dates to match your publishing schedule.

steps.heading: Open the Templates hub
steps.body: In the left sidebar, click Templates and select the Marketing category.

steps.heading: Choose an editorial template
steps.body: Look for the Editorial Calendar or Blog Content Schedule template in the marketing library.

steps.heading: Create your board from the template
steps.body: Click Use Template, name your board, choose your workspace, and keep checklists enabled.

steps.heading: Review the pre-built pipeline
steps.body: Check the default lanes, typically Ideas, Writing, Editing, Scheduled, and Published.

steps.heading: Customize cards and labels
steps.body: Open a sample card to fill in details like SEO keywords or draft text, and assign colors to labels for channels like Instagram or your blog.

steps.heading: Set dates and switch to Calendar view
steps.body: Add publish dates to your cards, then switch to the Calendar view from the header to see your monthly schedule at a glance.

faq.question: Do I have to build a content calendar from scratch?
faq.answer: No, Trello's Marketing template library includes pre-built editorial calendar templates you can duplicate and customize.

faq.question: What are the default stages in the editorial template?
faq.answer: The default lanes typically include Ideas, Writing, Editing, Scheduled, and Published.

faq.question: How do I track which channel a piece of content is for?
faq.answer: Use color-coded labels to represent different channels, like Instagram, YouTube, or your blog.

faq.question: Can I see my content schedule on a monthly calendar?
faq.answer: Yes, switching to the Calendar view lets you see publish dates laid out across the month.
